learning_outcomes.description: Explain and recognise suitable problems in data science and business analytics.
Explain the strategy and modelling for data science and business analytics projects.
Explain the main data analytic methods for solving data science and business analytics projects.
Explain how to use data analytic methods in real-life.
Demonstrate how business strategy based on data science and business analytics can inform and improve business performance.

assessments.due_date:
1/09/2026 4:00 pm

If you are unable to attend the simulation please advise your Course Coordinator on or before the date of the simulation.
assessments.task_description:
In pre-assigned groups of 3-4 students, you will be running a Food Truck Simulation. Each student has to submit a 1500-word reflection on the simulation -- this is an individual assessment.

PLEASE NOTE: The simulation will be run in Week 4 during class. All Students are REQUIRED to attend the class.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessment items to be uploaded to Blackboard as a PDF
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Presentations in class Week 11 - Week 12

Presentation Materials - 10:00AM - 12/10/2026

Presentation During class in Weeks 11 and Week 12. You are required to make in-person and in-class presentations. ALL PRESENTATION DECKS to be submitted by the due date indicated.
assessments.task_description:
Throughout the semester you will be designing a Smart product (AI- and Data-embedded product). You are required to present a suitable solution (Smart Product) to the problem identified using GenAI augmented Design Thinking. This will comprise 1) assessing the product-market fit using GenAI simulated Synthetic data, 2) Developing its GTM strategy, in particular the Business Model, and 3) the type of data and analytics deployed in this product.

Each group’s presentation will be 20 minutes long and will be presented in class and should be done via Powerpoint over Zoom to allow the recording of the presentations.

Students will be in groups of 4 (or 3 if a final group of 4 is not possible) and it is expected each student will present for 3-4 minutes (6.5 minutes in case there is a group of 3 students). Students will be presenting in the tutorial.

If you are unable to find a group of 4 to join, please let the course co-ordinator know and we will connect you with other students.

PLEASE NOTE: You will be expected to have sorted out your group by week 3.

Note that although the presentation is in a group, marks will be individual based on your part of the presentation.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
